I'm currently focused on ensuring all mobile action buttons match the height of the main tab bar ("Block 1"). Next, I plan to move my focus to blocks 2 and 3, examining their current code, to keep the UI uniform.


**Pinpointing UI Discrepancies**

I've identified the root cause of the height mismatch between the tabs and the recall/new buttons. The tabs use a combination of container padding and button padding (`p-1` and `py-2`), while the recall/new buttons use a single, larger padding value (`p-3`). The evidence actions in Block 2 are using explicit height values (`h-9` and `h-10`), and padding, which needs consideration for consistency. I'm focusing on these specific elements to standardize button dimensions.


**Matching Button Dimensions**

I've determined the ideal approach is to modify the padding on the "Recall" and "New" buttons from `p-3` to `p-2` to better visually align with the main tabs. I've also confirmed that the structure of the action buttons in Blocks 2 and 3 mirrors Block 2, with the same explicit height values to consider. The user's goal is uniformity across all mobile action buttons.


**Refining Mobile Button Style**

I'm now focusing on standardizing the mobile action buttons to the same height. I'll make sure they all use `h-10` or `p-2` instead of the current inconsistent values. Text will be hidden on mobile, and the buttons will be centered using Flexbox's `justify-center`. My goal is for all action buttons to be uniformly sized and icon-only on mobile, matching the overall design language.

I've determined that the "Recall" and "New" buttons need their padding reduced to `p-2` to match the tabs' height. My analysis of `App.tsx` confirms these discrepancies, which are vital to fix. I am now refining the evidence action buttons in Blocks 2 and 3 so they uniformly use `h-10` and are centered, which should also resolve any other inconsistencies, in line with my goal of a uniform mobile action button layout.


**Ensuring Uniform Button Style**

I'm now focusing on the action buttons across various sections (Findings, Report).  I'll standardize the height to `h-10` (40px) and width to either `w-10` or use `aspect-square` for purely icon-based designs on mobile. I'll make sure that all the text is hidden on mobile devices using `hidden md:inline`. To align button styling with the main tabs, the changes will address buttons' paddings in lines with Block 1's styling: `bg-gray-100 p-1 rounded-xl` and nested buttons' `px-1 py-2`. The "Recall/New" buttons will have their padding adjusted to `p-2`.


**Evaluating Mobile Action Buttons**

I'm now focusing on the action buttons across several sections: Findings, Report, and others. I'll make sure the `h-9 md:h-10` is consistently changed to `h-10`. I'm going to make sure the "Export Raw" and the others are uniform. In mobile, the Evidence actions are already centered using flexbox. The `justify-between` in findings actions are currently left-and-right, but I might make them icon-only and same height.


**Applying Tab Style**

I'm now integrating the "Block 1" styling, focusing on the absence of backgrounds for unselected buttons. I'll retain the standard background for the primary action button.  In `App.tsx`, I'll adjust the padding on the "Recall" and "New" buttons to `p-2`.  I'll also ensure all action buttons now use `h-10` and hide text on mobile (`hidden md:inline`). The "Export" buttons already meet these criteria.


**Improving Mobile Buttons**

I'm now honing in on the "Export Report" button within the Report view. I noticed the text is not currently hidden on mobile. I'll address this by wrapping the "Export Report" text in a span with the `hidden md:inline` class. Next, I'll review the Template upload button, making sure it only shows an icon on mobile for consistent button appearance.


**Standardizing the UI**

I'm now revising the 'Upload Template' button to align with the desired mobile-friendly UI. I need to make sure the text "Upload Template" is hidden on smaller screens, keeping with the icon-only approach for these buttons. This ensures consistency with the "Export Report" button and other standardized elements. I believe I've completed the initial goals, so I will perform a final review.


**Standardizing Icon Visibility**

My current focus is on standardizing the visibility of button text on mobile. In the code, I'm replacing the classes to ensure that text is hidden on small screens and shown on larger ones, strictly adhering to the "icon-only" approach. The `App.tsx` file is where I'm making these adjustments. I'm focusing on the code snippets you provided to ensure that all the buttons across the application align with this standard. I am nearly complete, and ready to perform a final check.


**Updating Mobile UI Buttons**

I'm now revising the 'Upload Template' button to use the `hidden md:inline` class to show the text only on medium screens and up. I've confirmed that the existing buttons within the Findings section correctly implement the `hidden md:inline` rule, maintaining the icon-only design on mobile. The Findings View Toggle is already adhering to the intended visibility settings. I am going to verify the height values one more time before final review, and then will begin the final testing, to ensure all buttons across the application align with the established standard.


**Revising Button Components**

I'm now integrating the style of Block 1's tabs into the action button containers within Block 2 and 3. My focus is on achieving uniformity, so I'll wrap the action buttons in containers styled with `bg-gray-100 p-1 rounded-xl`. I will make sure all text is hidden on mobile screens, retaining only icons for these buttons. This aligns with the Block 1 styling as the standard. I'm finalizing my updates to ensure these changes are reflected consistently throughout.


**Confirming Button Uniformity**

I've just finished the final check and confirmation, and I've ensured all action buttons now use `h-10` and hide text on mobile (`hidden md:inline`), with any necessary padding adjustments to maintain visual alignment. I've re-reviewed the `App.tsx` file and verified the changes, including the padding on the "Recall" and "New" buttons, and the styling of all action buttons within the evidence, findings, and report sections. The "Export Raw" button has been confirmed. I am ready to test my work and deliver the mobile-friendly UI.


**Standardizing Header Layout**

I've re-checked the "Analyze" button, confirming it will indeed display only the wand icon on mobile. After that, I verified the tab padding and revised the "Recall" and "New" button padding to p-2. These updates are meant to refine the overall mobile-friendliness of the UI and ensure uniformity.

**Revising UI Aesthetics**

I'm currently focused on the UI elements. My main concern is to enhance the visual appeal and clarity. I'm prioritizing color adjustments within the Findings block, aiming for better contrast. I'm also considering general tweaks to the Report block's visual elements, hoping to improve the user experience overall.

Font consistency is next; I'm standardizing the font in the Report block to match the Evidence block, making it `font-mono`. I'm also addressing the "List View" / "Matrix View" button text, making sure it displays correctly.


**Prioritizing Mobile Display**

I'm now assessing how well the UI renders on mobile devices. I'm focusing on ensuring text visibility and an effective layout, especially considering the limited screen space. This involves fine-tuning font sizes and spacing to optimize readability.


**Optimizing UI Updates**

My latest focus has been on improving the user experience during updates. I've removed the overly generic "scroll to bottom" behavior. Now, I'm working on a system to scroll directly to the relevant element index using `findingRefs`. I'm also addressing the loading text style, reducing its size to `text-lg` and ensuring it's centered with appropriate padding. Finally, I'm standardizing the font in the report section, shifting the textarea from serif to monospace.


**Matching Font Styles**

I've just ensured that the font in the Report section now uses `font-mono`, matching the Evidence block. I've also refactored the View Toggle buttons to display "List" and "Matrix" effectively, even on small screens. I'm focusing on ensuring the container adapts without layout breakage. I've removed the generic auto-scroll behavior, replacing it with direct scrolling to the relevant element using `findingRefs`.


**Clarifying Scrolling Logic**

I'm zeroing in on the scrolling behavior. It seems the problem stems from the `useEffect` trigger on `analysisResult` for *all* edits, not just additions.  I need to refine the scroll-to-bottom logic, triggering it only when `analysisResult`'s length changes, indicating a new item. Immediate scrolling with `refs.current[index + 1]` after edits should be safe given the asynchronous nature of React state updates and how `handleUpdateFinding` operates. I'll need to update `App.tsx` and integrate the refactor to control the scroll-to-bottom specifically for new items only.


**Fine-tuning Component Logic**

I've just made adjustments to the React components. I've modified the `useEffect` dependency to `[analysisResult?.length]` to refine the scroll behavior, ensuring it triggers only on additions. I've also integrated the scroll logic into the `handleUpdateFinding` function and updated the Findings block's `className` for color adjustments. The Report section now has `font-mono text-sm` and the footer's list/matrix text is simplified. The AINeuralLoader's styling has also been updated, reducing the font size and enhancing the loading message's container. I am now working on generating the XML block.


**Improving Text Readability**

I'm currently focused on enhancing text contrast and readability. I've adjusted the color scheme, ensuring the text is easily readable against the background. I'm also ensuring the scroll behavior works smoothly within the `handleUpdateFinding` function, scrolling to the next finding when the status is updated. Further, I am focusing on standardizing the font and improving the loading text.

To address your requests, I will modify `App.tsx` and `components/UI.tsx`.

**Summary of Changes:**

1.  **Findings Block Colors:** Updated the card styling in `layoutMode === 'findings'` to use softer backgrounds with higher contrast borders and text, ensuring readability in both Light and Dark modes.
2.  **Auto-Scroll Behavior:**
    *   Removed the `useEffect` that forced scrolling to the bottom on *every* change. It now only scrolls to the bottom when new items are added (length changes).
    *   Added logic to `handleUpdateFinding`: When the `status` is changed, it automatically scrolls the *next* finding into view (`block: 'center'`), creating a smooth workflow.
3.  **Loading Text:** Updated `AINeuralLoader` in `components/UI.tsx` to use smaller text (`text-lg`), added horizontal padding, and max-width to prevent edge-touching.
4.  **Report Font:** Changed the Report `textarea` class from `font-serif` to `font-mono text-sm` to match the Evidence block exactly.
5.  **View Toggle Buttons:** Simplified the button layout to ensure "List" and "Matrix" text displays correctly without cramping.

export const Icon = ({ name, size = 18, className = "", onClick }: { name: string, size?: number, className?: string, onClick?: (e: any) => void }) => {
    const iconNode = Icons[name] || Icons['AlertCircle']; // Fallback
    
    if (React.isValidElement(iconNode)) {
        const element = iconNode as React.ReactElement<any>;
         return React.cloneElement(element, {
            width: size,
            height: size,
            className: `${className} ${element.props.className || ''}`,
            onClick

};

export const CommandPaletteModal = ({ isOpen, onClose, actions, onSelectAction }: any) => {
    const [search, setSearch] = useState("");
    const inputRef = useRef<HTMLInputElement>(null);

    useEffect(() => {
        if (isOpen) {
            setSearch("");
            setTimeout(() => inputRef.current?.focus(), 50);
        }
    }, [isOpen]);

    const filteredActions = actions.filter((a: any) => 
        a.label.toLowerCase().includes(search.toLowerCase()) || 
        (a.desc && a.desc.toLowerCase().includes(search.toLowerCase()))
    );

    if (!isOpen) return null;

    return (
        <div className="fixed inset-0 z-[99999] bg-black/60 backdrop-blur-sm flex items-start justify-center pt-[15vh] animate-in fade-in duration-200" onClick={onClose}>
            <div 
                className="w-full max-w-2xl bg-white dark:bg-slate-900 rounded-2xl shadow-2xl border border-gray-200 dark:border-slate-700 overflow-hidden flex flex-col animate-zoom-in-spring duration-300"
                onClick={e => e.stopPropagation()}
            >
                <div className="flex items-center px-4 py-3 border-b border-gray-100 dark:border-slate-800">
                    <Icon name="Search" className="text-slate-400 mr-3" size={20} />
                    <input 
                        ref={inputRef}
                        type="text" 
                        className="flex-1 bg-transparent text-lg outline-none text-slate-800 dark:text-white placeholder-slate-400"
                        placeholder="Type a command or search clauses..."
                        value={search}
                        onChange={e => setSearch(e.target.value)}
                        onKeyDown={e => {
                            if (e.key === 'Enter' && filteredActions.length > 0) {
                                onSelectAction(filteredActions[0]);
                            } else if (e.key === 'Escape') {
                                onClose();
                            }
                        }}
                    />
                    <div className="flex gap-2">
                        <span className="text-[10px] bg-gray-100 dark:bg-slate-800 text-slate-500 px-2 py-1 rounded border border-gray-200 dark:border-slate-700">ESC</span>
                    </div>
                </div>
                <div className="max-h-[60vh] overflow-y-auto custom-scrollbar p-2">
                    {filteredActions.length === 0 ? (
                        <div className="p-8 text-center text-slate-500">No results found.</div>
                    ) : (
                        filteredActions.map((action: any, idx: number) => (
                            <div 
                                key={idx}
                                onClick={() => onSelectAction(action)}
                                className={`flex items-center gap-4 p-3 rounded-xl cursor-pointer transition-colors group ${idx === 0 ? 'bg-indigo-50 dark:bg-indigo-900/20' : 'hover:bg-gray-50 dark:hover:bg-slate-800'}`}
                            >
                                <div className={`p-2 rounded-lg ${action.type === 'clause' ? 'bg-blue-100 text-blue-600 dark:bg-blue-900/30 dark:text-blue-400' : 'bg-gray-100 text-slate-600 dark:bg-slate-800 dark:text-slate-400'}`}>
                                    <Icon name={action.icon || "Session6_Zap"} size={18}/>
                                </div>
                                <div className="flex-1 min-w-0">
                                    <h4 className="text-sm font-bold text-slate-800 dark:text-slate-200 truncate group-hover:text-indigo-600 dark:group-hover:text-indigo-400 transition-colors">
                                        {action.label}
                                    </h4>
                                    {action.desc && <p className="text-xs text-slate-500 truncate">{action.desc}</p>}
                                </div>
                                {action.shortcut && <span className="text-xs text-slate-400">{action.shortcut}</span>}
                            </div>
                        ))
                    )}
                </div>
                <div className="px-4 py-2 bg-gray-50 dark:bg-slate-950 border-t border-gray-100 dark:border-slate-800 text-[10px] text-slate-400 flex justify-between">
                     <span><strong>Tip:</strong> Search for clauses like "9.2" or actions like "Export".</span>
                     <span>ISO Audit Pro</span>
                </div>
            </div>
        </div>
    );
};

export const FontSizeController = ({ fontSizeScale, adjustFontSize }: any) => {
    const [isOpen, setIsOpen] = useState(false);
    // SNAPSHOT COORDINATES: Captured once when opened, never updated by re-renders.
    const [pinnedPosition, setPinnedPosition] = useState<{ top: number, right: number } | null>(null);
    const buttonRef = useRef<HTMLButtonElement>(null);

    const handleToggle = (e: React.MouseEvent) => {
        // Stop propagation to prevent immediate closing if using global click listener
        e.stopPropagation();
        
        if (!isOpen && buttonRef.current) {
            // CAPTURE CURRENT VIEWPORT POSITION
            const rect = buttonRef.current.getBoundingClientRect();
            // Calculate fixed position relative to viewport
            // We align the right edge of the popup with the right edge of the button
            const rightEdge = window.innerWidth - rect.right;
            setPinnedPosition({
                top: rect.bottom + 8,
                right: rightEdge
            });
            setIsOpen(true);
        } else {
            setIsOpen(false);
        }
    };

    return (
        <>
            <button 
                ref={buttonRef}
                onClick={handleToggle} 
                className={`p-2 rounded-xl transition-all duration-300 shadow-sm ${isOpen ? 'bg-indigo-100 text-indigo-700 dark:bg-slate-700 dark:text-indigo-400 scale-105' : 'bg-gray-50 dark:bg-slate-800 text-slate-700 dark:text-slate-200 hover:bg-indigo-50 hover:text-indigo-600'}`}
                title="Adjust Font Size"
            >
                <Icon name="TextSize" size={18}/>
            </button>
            
            {/* FIXED PORTAL-LIKE OVERLAY */}
            {isOpen && pinnedPosition && (
                <>
                    {/* Invisible Backdrop to handle click-outside */}
                    <div 
                        className="fixed inset-0 z-[99] cursor-default" 
                        onClick={() => setIsOpen(false)}
                    ></div>

                    {/* THE STATIC CONTROL PANEL */}
                    <div 
                        className="fixed z-[100] flex items-center bg-white dark:bg-slate-900 rounded-full p-1.5 shadow-2xl border border-gray-200 dark:border-slate-700 animate-zoom-in-spring min-w-[140px] justify-between"
                        style={{
                            top: `${pinnedPosition.top}px`,
                            right: `${pinnedPosition.right}px`,
                            // Ensure it stays "glued" to screen glass coordinates
                        }}
                        onClick={(e) => e.stopPropagation()} // Prevent backdrop click from closing when clicking inside
                    >
                         <button onClick={() => adjustFontSize('decrease')} className="p-2 hover:bg-gray-100 dark:hover:bg-slate-800 rounded-full text-slate-500 hover:text-indigo-600 transition-all duration-200 flex-shrink-0 active:scale-90"><Icon name="Minus" size={16}/></button>
                         <span className="text-sm font-black text-slate-800 dark:text-white select-none px-2 w-12 text-center">{(fontSizeScale * 100).toFixed(0)}%</span>
                         <button onClick={() => adjustFontSize('increase')} className="p-2 hover:bg-gray-100 dark:hover:bg-slate-800 rounded-full text-slate-500 hover:text-indigo-600 transition-all duration-200 flex-shrink-0 active:scale-90"><Icon name="Plus" size={16}/></button>
                    </div>
                </>
            )}
        </>
    );
};

export const SparkleLoader = ({ className }: any) => (
    <div className={`flex items-center justify-center gap-1 ${className}`}>

export const SnowOverlay = () => {
    const flakes = Array.from({ length: 50 }).map((_, i) => ({
        left: Math.random() * 100,
        duration: Math.random() * 10 + 10, 
        delay: Math.random() * 10,
        size: Math.random() * 4 + 2, 
        opacity: Math.random() * 0.4 + 0.1,
    }));

    return (
        <div className="fixed inset-0 pointer-events-none z-[99999] overflow-hidden select-none">
            {flakes.map((f, i) => (
                <div
                    key={i}
                    className="absolute bg-white rounded-full blur-[1px]"
                    style={{
                        left: `${f.left}%`,
                        top: -10,
                        width: `${f.size}px`,
                        height: `${f.size}px`,
                        opacity: f.opacity,
                        animation: `fall ${f.duration}s linear infinite`,
                        animationDelay: `-${f.delay}s`,
                    }}
                />
            ))}
            <style>{`
                @keyframes fall {
                    0% { transform: translateY(-10vh) translateX(0px); opacity: 0; }
                    10% { opacity: 1; }
                    50% { transform: translateY(50vh) translateX(20px); }
                    100% { transform: translateY(110vh) translateX(-20px); opacity: 0; }
                }
            `}</style>
        </div>
    );
};

export const Toast = ({ message, onClose }: { message: string, onClose: () => void }) => {
    const [isExiting, setIsExiting] = useState(false);

    const handleClose = () => {
        setIsExiting(true);
        setTimeout(() => {
            onClose();
        }, 300); // Match animation duration
    };
    
    useEffect(() => {
        const timer = setTimeout(handleClose, 3700);
        return () => clearTimeout(timer);
    }, [onClose]);

    return (
        <div className={`fixed bottom-6 left-1/2 transform -translate-x-1/2 z-[300] transition-all duration-300 ease-soft ${isExiting ? 'opacity-0 translate-y-4' : 'opacity-100 translate-y-0'}`}>
            <div className="bg-slate-900 dark:bg-white text-white dark:text-slate-900 px-6 py-3 rounded-2xl shadow-2xl flex items-center gap-4 border border-slate-700 dark:border-slate-200">
                <div className="p-1 bg-green-500 rounded-full text-white">
                    <Icon name="CheckLineart" size={14}/>
                </div>
                <div className="flex flex-col">
                    <span className="text-xs font-black uppercase tracking-wider opacity-70">Notification</span>
                    <span className="text-sm font-bold">{message}</span>
                </div>
                <button onClick={handleClose} className="ml-2 text-slate-500 hover:text-white dark:hover:text-slate-900 transition-colors">
                    <Icon name="X" size={16}/>
                </button>
            </div>
        </div>
    );
};

**Revising Button Logic**

I'm considering scenarios where the button should be disabled, like when the analysis hasn't produced any useful output. The current implementation seems inconsistent, so I'm working to refine the conditions that govern its state.


**Updating Synthesis Conditions**

I'm rethinking the "Synthesize" button's behavior based on user feedback. The goal is a more flexible approach, mirroring the "Analyze" button's enablement. Previously, it relied heavily on `analysisResult`, but I'm now incorporating a broader criteria: "Synthesize" will be active if *Evidence* or *Findings* are available, even if analysis is incomplete. It's about having enough material to create a report, aligning with the "Evidence + Finding (If any)" concept. This should make the functionality more intuitive.


**Rethinking Report Generation**

I've been honing the "Generate Report" button's logic. It's no longer tied solely to `analysisResult`. Now, the button activates with either existing `evidence` or a combination of `analysisResult` and `selectedFindings`. The disable condition is now  `!hasEvidence && (!analysisResult || Object.values(selectedFindings).every(v => !v))`. I'm also addressing the `handleGenerateReport` function to account for missing `analysisResult` but present `evidence`. Lastly, I'm focusing on mobile UI improvements, specifically centering footer buttons within the "Findings" block on smaller screens.


**Implementing UI Grouping**

I'm focused on the "Findings" block's mobile UI. Specifically, I'm adjusting the footer buttons' layout to be grouped and centered. I've located the relevant `div` in `App.tsx` and am analyzing the current classes: `flex-shrink-0 flex items-center gap-2 mt-2 w-full overflow-x-auto no-scrollbar justify-center md:justify-between`. To group them, I'll need to modify the spacing and alignment, likely reducing the gap and centering the elements regardless of screen size. The `md:justify-between` is the culprit for the current behavior; replacing this should enable the desired grouping on smaller screens.


**Optimizing Button Placement**

I'm now revising the footer button grouping logic. Given my previous efforts, the buttons are centered, but the user prefers *grouping*. I'm now fine-tuning the `justify-` properties to center and group buttons based on screen size, ensuring a proper layout on mobile devices. I'll maintain the centering behavior on smaller screens, and employ the original layout on larger screens, to address the desired functionality while respecting screen real-estate. I'm focusing on the responsive styling of the container to achieve the targeted visual hierarchy.


**Refactoring Report Prompts**

I'm now revising the report generation prompt construction.  I'm focusing on incorporating `evidence` and `acceptedFindings` seamlessly.  I've replaced the direct evidence and finding insertion, adapting to `hasTemplate` conditions. This includes ensuring proper context inclusion for both cases and verifying that the correct strings are constructed for the API call. The goal is cleaner, more context-rich prompts. I'm checking the impact on different report scenarios.


**Fine-tuning Button Grouping**

### 1. Nâng cấp Tra cứu Clause (Reference)
*   **Prompt Kỹ thuật mới**: Yêu cầu AI trả về văn bản có cấu trúc phân cấp (Hierarchy). Sử dụng Markdown chuẩn để phân tách các mục `a, b, c` rõ ràng.

*   Tích hợp hàm kiểm soát chất lượng dữ liệu trước khi parse JSON và trước khi lưu file, đảm bảo cấu trúc dữ liệu không bị lỗi "luông tuồng".
